title: ‘They won't give it to us, we'll take it!’
description: Young people can achieve the life they want only by fighting for their rights. No force will come down from above and grant us the life we want; throughout history, rights have never been won that way. We will achieve this. They won't give us what we demand, so we will take it. We will take it with our actions, our stance, and our fighting spirit. Those in power want to see young people as an obedient, unquestioning, passive generation. We, however, want to be subjects; I believe we have shown that we are not only capable of wanting this, but also of being a political subject that can set the country's agenda. This is where the fundamental divergence begins. While we defend our future, our universities, and our lives, the system that threatens each of these imposes apathy upon us.

title: US to stop collecting 'illegal' Trump tariffs from tomorrow after Supreme Court ruling
description: Even though the Supreme Court ruled on Friday that President Donald Trump’s “reciprocal” tariffs were illegal, US importers continued to pay duties on goods entering the country, given the CBP had not yet updated its Cargo System Management Service to eliminate the tariffs imposed under the IEEPA.

title: The S&P 500 Trails the Global Stock Market by Its Widest Margin Since 1995 as President Trump's Policies Rattle Investors
description: The global stock market has more than doubled the S&P 500's return since January 2025.

title: Who Is Raising Our Children Now, Parents or The Streets?
description: There is a quiet grief many parents and elders carry today. It does not shout or trend, but it lingers—each time teenagers behave like adults before learning how to be children, each time values are replaced with fleeting fashions, and each time society applauds what it once corrected. What we are witnessing is not merely [...]
